explanation: This command is a direct representation of the true positive test case. It uses mshta.exe with a vbscript payload to execute a PowerShell script, which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.

This command uses encoded and quoted variations to bypass simple string-based detection. It simulates a real-world scenario where attackers might obfuscate payloads to evade detection while still triggering the Sigma rule.

This command simplifies the original payload slightly while maintaining the same execution pattern. It uses mshta.exe with a VBScript payload to execute a PowerShell command, which should still trigger the Sigma rule.
